nodes.name: anki_import_command.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the supported live Anki release boundary: preflight backup, EAVM model migration, controlled APKG identity growth, note/media synchronization, and post-import verification through AnkiConnect. Existing note identities and schedules are preserved while only missing canonical identities may be added.

nodes.name: _chunks
nodes.summary: Partitions Anki note or card IDs into bounded request batches.

nodes.name: _require_not_false
nodes.summary: Rejects direct mutation failures and failed nested actions inside AnkiConnect multi-response envelopes.

nodes.name: _template_state
nodes.summary: Accepts only a safe single-template legacy layout or the exact ordered Recognition/Production template layout.

nodes.name: _model_contract
nodes.summary: Validates immutable EAVM model identity, rejects foreign or suffixed collisions, and accepts only sanctioned append-only field prefixes and template layouts.

nodes.name: load_expected_signatures
nodes.summary: Loads a multiset of exact canonical Anki field signatures from the build JSONL for live note comparison.

nodes.name: load_expected_media
nodes.summary: Collects every headword and example-audio filename referenced through sound markers or HTML audio elements in the build JSONL.

nodes.name: _identity
nodes.summary: Builds stable Card Identity from Word, PartOfSpeech, CEFRLevel, and the reviewed SecondarySense or SenseVariant tags that distinguish sibling identities.

nodes.name: load_expected_records
nodes.summary: Indexes canonical notes by reviewed Card Identity and retains their target deck, complete fields, tags, GUID, and Production-card eligibility.

nodes.name: _field_value
nodes.summary: Reads a string value from Anki's nested field payload while rejecting malformed field structures.

nodes.name: _card_schedule
nodes.summary: Captures the scheduling tuple whose byte-for-byte logical preservation is checked for established cards.

nodes.name: _load_cards_info
nodes.summary: Loads complete, unique Anki card records in batches and proves every requested card ID was returned exactly once.

nodes.name: snapshot_existing_collection
nodes.summary: Proves every existing EAVM note is a unique member of the canonical identity set, rejects stray cards or eligibility/deck drift, and snapshots note identities and schedules before mutation. The snapshot deliberately permits a canonical subset so a later validated APKG import can add missing identities.

nodes.name: validate_local_inputs
nodes.summary: Validates the APKG, canonical notes JSONL, safe media basenames, and presence of every referenced local audio file without contacting Anki.

nodes.name: _live_signature
nodes.summary: Converts a live Anki note into the exact ordered EAVM field signature used for canonical comparison.

nodes.name: sync_example_audio_fields
nodes.summary: Matches legacy notes by their immutable 15-field signature and batch-populates the append-only EAVM fields without duplicating identities.

nodes.name: sync_missing_media
nodes.summary: Compares canonical and live media SHA-256 values, then uploads every missing or stale referenced file in bounded AnkiConnect mutations.

nodes.name: _sha256_path
nodes.summary: Streams a local file into a SHA-256 digest for media byte attestation.

nodes.name: _canonical_media_hashes
nodes.summary: Resolves referenced media beneath the canonical audio root and hashes each verified local file.

nodes.name: _retrieve_live_media_hashes
nodes.summary: Retrieves live Anki media through versioned multi-actions, validates result envelopes and base64, and returns content hashes with optional missing-file tolerance.

nodes.name: _verify_live_media_bytes
nodes.summary: Requires every referenced live media file to match its canonical local bytes after synchronization.

nodes.name: sync_existing_notes
nodes.summary: Updates fields, tags, and sibling-card deck routing for existing canonical identities. Optional incomplete coverage supports the pre-import phase when the live deck is a proven subset of the build.

nodes.name: verify_import
nodes.summary: Fails closed unless the live model, templates, CSS, notes, GUID identities, cards, decks, and media exactly match the canonical build. For identity growth it proves established note IDs, identities, schedules, and cards remain intact while all and only missing identities appear as pristine cards.

nodes.name: preflight_and_backup
nodes.summary: Checks AnkiConnect and live model/deck compatibility, rejects stray cards, and exports a schedule-preserving backup before any mutation.

nodes.name: migrate_established_eavm_fields
nodes.summary: Appends only missing EAVM fields in canonical order to a correctly identified established model, then verifies the complete field contract.

nodes.name: sync_model_design
nodes.summary: Renames a legacy Recognition template in place, appends Production when needed, synchronizes exact templates and CSS, and verifies the live design.

nodes.name: import_and_verify
nodes.summary: Coordinates provenance and archive checks, migration, direct synchronization, media repair, GUID proof, and receipt publication. A non-empty established deck invokes native APKG import only when its proven canonical identities are a strict subset, allowing missing GUID-bearing notes to be added safely.

Push and manual runs are concurrency-cancelled and finish by asserting that tracked files remain unchanged.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Operational handbook for building, auditing, validating, packaging, and importing the IELTS deck. It defines repository ownership, subagent and test conventions, release boundaries, and hard-earned scraper and EAVM constraints.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Canonical project glossary defining EAVM card concepts, semantic review and registry language, release boundaries, data-source terms, and scraper architecture. It owns stable vocabulary while leaving implementation workflow and design rationale to other authorities.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses definition-and-avoid pairs as a controlled-language pattern that prevents terminology drift across code, data, and documentation.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Concise project overview and developer quick start for installing, testing, running the production pipeline, generating Example Audio, and importing through AnkiConnect. It directs readers to AGENTS.md and CONTEXT.md for detailed operations and canonical terminology.

nodes.name: USER_NOTES.md
nodes.summary: Chronological provenance ledger of direct user requests and semantic or release decisions from July 2026. It records historical intent rather than current authority, deferring current state to canonical ledgers, tests, ADRs, CONTEXT.md, and AGENTS.md.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python packaging and test configuration for the ielts-deck project, declaring Python 3.10+, runtime and test dependencies, the anki-english pipeline entry point, setuptools package discovery, and pytest defaults.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Human-readable pip requirements mirror of the dependencies declared in pyproject.toml, grouped by scraping, NLP, audio, packaging, validation, asynchronous I/O, review workbooks, testing, and parser support. It also records the required post-install NLTK corpus downloads.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the EAVM recognition and production card sources, packaging and Anki synchronization workflow, aligned bilingual/audio/collocation fields, CSS source-of-truth rule, and embedded-JavaScript newline constraint.
nodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese operational documentation uses English Anki, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript terminology where those names are contractual.

nodes.name: back_template.txt
nodes.summary: Defines the Recognition answer template, rendering aligned English/Vietnamese senses, examples, source-aware collocations, bilingual idioms, word-family metadata, dictionary links, and manual UK/US example audio controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Anki Mustache fields are combined with HTML and ES5-style DOM scripting so the template remains compatible with embedded Anki webviews.

nodes.name: front_template.txt
nodes.summary: Defines the Recognition question template with the headword, CEFR badge, linked POS chips, corpus badges, dictionary navigation, and a JavaScript-derived multi-sense indicator.
nodes.languageNotes: The text asset embeds Anki Mustache conditionals, HTML, and conservative browser JavaScript rather than being plain prose.

nodes.name: production_answer_prefix.txt
nodes.summary: Provides the Production answer prefix that wraps the rendered front side and inserts the answer divider before reusing the unchanged Recognition back template.
nodes.languageNotes: This minimal HTML fragment relies on Anki's special FrontSide field to compose the sibling card answer.

nodes.name: production_front_template.txt
nodes.summary: Defines the ordinal-1 Vietnamese-to-English Production prompt, aligning Vietnamese glosses with safely clozed examples, collapsing extra examples, preserving learning-pattern slots, and using Anki's native typed-answer comparison.
nodes.languageNotes: Nested Anki Mustache guards gate card generation, while embedded JavaScript derives safe lexical anchors and masks examples without hiding grammatical slots.

nodes.name: styling.txt
nodes.summary: Contains the generated production CSS for Recognition and Production cards, including typography, CEFR/POS/corpus chips, sense layouts, collocation provenance, idioms, audio state, responsive behavior, animations, and typed-answer feedback.
nodes.languageNotes: This CSS artifact is derived from the canonical card-style region in design/index.html and includes reduced-motion and narrow-screen adaptations.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative guide to the EAVM design system, its five-region HTML reference, design tokens, immutable selector contracts, and CSS synchronization workflow. It also records current card rules for bilingual meanings, collocation provenance, Production Cards, Sense Sorting, and Card Identity.

nodes.name: card-improvement-notes.md
nodes.summary: Design audit and prioritized backlog of ten non-authoritative EAVM improvement candidates covering responsive Sense Rows, idiom disclosure, accessibility, offline assets, preview-only CSS, and visual regression fixtures. Each candidate records an observation, a design question, a recommendation, and verification ideas.

nodes.name: card-improvement-user-notes.md
nodes.summary: Structured provenance log of user-supplied card improvement ideas, clarifying questions, implementation guidance, and status updates. Its entries cover reviewed identity splits, sense grouping, idiom selection and bilingual glosses, and missing vocabulary data.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Single-file visual source of truth for the EAVM card design, organized into design tokens, the sync-enforced production CSS region, component previews, representative sample cards, and Oxford label reference data. The bounded card-style block is mirrored into EAVM styling and guarded against drift.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a static single-page HTML and CSS catalog with explicit boundary comments separating production-synchronized card styles from preview-only reference regions.

nodes.name: sense_grouping_review.md
nodes.summary: Decision ledger for learner-facing Sense Row grouping, separation, removal, and identity-variant reviews across named vocabulary cards. It preserves distinct source senses for provenance while documenting how approved meanings and examples should appear in the deck.

nodes.name: 0001-lxml-parser-backend.md
nodes.summary: Records the evidence-backed choice of lxml with cssselect for Oxford and Cambridge parsing, including benchmark results, source-faithful text behavior, golden-fixture equivalence, and rejected alternatives.

nodes.name: 0002-multi-pos-merge-bug.md
nodes.summary: Documents the historical Oxford multi-POS sense-duplication bug, its page-boundary root cause and scale, the interim workaround, and the planned section-aware parser correction.

nodes.name: 0003-colloc-artifact-filter.md
nodes.summary: Records removal of ellipsis artifacts from Oxford collocations and the deterministic record sort that prevents parallel parse order from changing merge-layer primary-homonym metadata.

nodes.name: 0005-gloss-pipeline-rendering.md
nodes.summary: Preserves the extensive history of the legacy gloss pipeline: sense separators, structural validation, reviewed rule taxonomies, reruns, precision phrases, and later audit refinements now superseded for semantic ownership by ADR 0011.
nodes.languageNotes: This long ADR is intentionally cumulative: later dated addenda amend earlier rules, and ADR 0011 supersedes its final Definition/Example ownership while retaining historical mechanics.

nodes.name: 0006-registry-driven-fail-closed-build.md
nodes.summary: Establishes Card Registry and Manual Card Payloads as canonical build inputs with strict identity validation, staged JSONL/TXT parity checks, deterministic ordering, and transactional publication.

nodes.name: 0007-reviewed-pos-split-variants.md
nodes.summary: Defines reviewed noun/verb Card Identity splits that preserve the existing noun GUID, add a verb GUID, and remain allowlisted exceptions; its amendment reverses the torture split while retaining trail.

nodes.name: 0008-learning-pattern-headwords.md
nodes.summary: Allows reviewed learning-pattern headwords such as derive from and devote sth to sth while preserving GUIDs and mapping display phrases back to base lemmas for source, audio, and corpus lookup.

nodes.name: 0009-retire-one-shot-executables.md
nodes.summary: Requires completed one-shot migration and review commands to leave HEAD once canonical outputs and durable regressions preserve their results, with Git history serving as the audit archive.

nodes.name: 0010-content-addressed-example-audio.md
nodes.summary: Defines the separate resumable Edge TTS stage, versioned content-addressed UK/US example audio, committed media validation, appended EAVM fields, manual playback, and AnkiConnect-only import boundary.

nodes.name: 0011-bilingual-semantic-registry-cutover.md
nodes.summary: Cuts production Definition and Example ownership over from legacy beta/gamma/M3 layers to one complete reviewed bilingual Semantic Registry promoted deterministically from a fail-closed audit ledger.

nodes.name: 0012-parallel-semantic-audit-manifests.md
nodes.summary: Defines deterministic GUID-level partitioning, weighted worker assignment, fingerprinted manifests, isolated worker bundle directories, and coordinator-only application for parallel semantic review.

nodes.name: 0013-dictionary-link-provenance.md
nodes.summary: Requires Oxford links to come from per-POS canonical source URLs and Semantic Source IDs, derives Cambridge links from normalized lemmas, appends aligned EAVM fields, and degrades ambiguity to non-clickable UI.

nodes.name: 0014-eavm-production-card.md
nodes.summary: Adds a Vietnamese-to-English Production sibling at ordinal 1 on the established EAVM note type, derives a typed answer after all transforms, gates eligibility on complete fields, and preserves live schedules through AnkiConnect migration.

nodes.name: 0015-bilingual-idiom-semantic-payload.md
nodes.summary: Defines phrase-level bilingual idiom review, display-mode semantics, fingerprinted complete coverage, Semantic Registry ownership, aligned IdiomMeaningVI rendering, and preservation of selected examples and audio.

nodes.name: 0016-vietnamese-definition-naturalness-review.md
nodes.summary: Introduces fingerprint-bound human review of long Vietnamese definitions as triage rather than a hard limit, with transactional rewrites that preserve semantic identity and registry ownership; ADR 0019 later broadens promotion coverage.

nodes.name: 0017-reviewed-learner-relevance-filter.md
nodes.summary: Defines a reviewed, source-accounted Learner Relevance Filter for removing narrow senses without numeric caps or automatic domain-label heuristics, followed by deterministic no-cap Sense Sorting.

nodes.name: 0018-reviewed-bilingual-lexical-gloss-concision.md
nodes.summary: Defines independently authored English and Vietnamese Lexical Glosses, heuristic-only length queues, and counterfactual evidence requirements for retaining explanatory wording without unsafe automatic compression.

nodes.name: 0019-all-sense-vietnamese-naturalness-gate.md
nodes.summary: Expands Vietnamese Naturalness Review to every promoted Semantic Sense with fingerprint reuse, explicit approved verdicts, natural lexical equivalents, and stricter long-gloss evidence while keeping Semantic Registry as sole owner.

nodes.name: 0020-pos-scoped-idiom-ownership.md
nodes.summary: Moves idiom ownership to Oxford entry POS, preserves phrase/POS identity through merge, filters before display ranking, and requires regeneration of all downstream semantic, audio, package, and live artifacts.

nodes.name: 0021-semantic-policy-and-concision-promotion-gates.md
nodes.summary: Adds machine-readable semantic policy locks plus exact-coverage Definition Concision and Sense Merge review ledgers, enforcing user wording invariants and row-specific evidence as mandatory promotion inputs.

nodes.name: 0022-package-provenance-and-release-guard.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision establishing content-bound Package Provenance, post-import receipts, byte-level archive and live verification, and three read-only Release Guard scopes. It separates canonical, package, and live-import evidence so stale artifacts cannot authorize a release.

nodes.name: 0023-canonical-guid-and-live-export-proof.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision requiring canonical base91 GUID storage, strict TSV serialization, byte-exact media checks, and post-import AnkiConnect exports as Live GUID Proof. It prefers modern collection.anki21 data and rejects unsupported formats instead of trusting note counts or absent API fields.

nodes.name: 0024-reviewed-collocation-registry.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision introducing a fingerprint-bound, item-level Collocation Audit and standalone Collocation Registry for reviewed learner-facing phrases and provenance. It distinguishes mandatory example-linked evidence from supporting candidates, enforces a five-chip budget, and exposes Oxford or Cambridge sources without automatic promotion.

nodes.name: 0025-pos-scoped-oxford-opal-membership.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision storing Oxford OPAL Written and Spoken membership as a deterministic POS-keyed map instead of a word-level scalar. It defines entry-scoped badge extraction, per-POS merging and folding, final-card tag application, and ambiguity guards that prevent cross-POS leakage.

nodes.name: 0026-entry-scoped-pronunciation-manifest.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision making each accent's IPA and audio an indivisible dictionary-entry selection, with explicit locks for ambiguity, aliases, and reviewed absence. A generated Headword Audio Manifest separately attests selection identity, media identity, filenames, hashes, and local bytes for fail-closed builds.

nodes.name: 0027-atomic-card-identity-split.md
nodes.summary: Architecture decision defining a fingerprint-bound transaction for splitting one reviewed Card Identity into stable primary and secondary cards while preserving complete Semantic Source Coverage. Review bundles, staged validation, durable journals, rollback, recovery, and idempotency prevent partially published registry and semantic-review state.

nodes.name: golden_cambridge_v2.json
nodes.summary: Golden expected parser output for five Cambridge HTML fixtures covering fifteen definitions across outbreak, necessarily, engineering, transparent, and donation. The records exercise normalized source metadata, pronunciation, CEFR, examples, collocations, and related lexical fields for regression tests.

nodes.name: golden_oxford_v2.json
nodes.summary: Golden expected parser output for five Oxford HTML fixtures covering seventeen definitions across linger, kilometre, dispose, remote, and delight. The records capture Oxford-specific list badges, sense numbering, topics, verb forms, collocation evidence, idioms, and polymorphic cache metadata for regression tests.

nodes.name: parser_fixture_manifest.json

Each special fixture declares source-aware slug resolution and semantic assertions for words, POS sections, definition counts, idiom ownership, see-also entries, collocation evidence, or OPAL membership.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand why this repository exists: it turns dictionary evidence and reviewed bilingual semantics into a production IELTS and Academic English Anki deck. Its quick start also introduces the scrape-to-import workflow that organizes the rest of this tour.

tour.title: Pipeline and Paths
tour.description: With the project goal established, the production pipeline shows how scrape, Example Audio, build, validation, packaging, and verified import execute as ordered stages. The shared configuration anchors every canonical source, review, build, media, design, and release path, while pyproject.toml exposes the installed command and test defaults.
tour.languageLesson: Python packaging entry points map an installed shell command to a module callable, while module execution keeps the same orchestration available through `python -m`.

tour.title: Vocabulary Authorities
tour.description: Before the pipeline can collect dictionary evidence, these Oxford and Academic Word List documents define the vocabulary population in scope. The Cambridge fallback configuration adds dated, source-attributed POS and CEFR reconciliation without replacing those canonical seed authorities.

tour.title: Dictionary Ingestion
tour.description: The vocabulary authorities feed source-specific Oxford and Cambridge parsers that normalize senses, examples, pronunciation, labels, idioms, and collocation evidence. Their records are merged without losing multi-POS evidence, then published deterministically by the cache rebuild command. The parser ADR explains why lxml and source-faithful golden behavior underpin this layer.

tour.title: Canonical Identity Model
tour.description: Once raw dictionary evidence exists, stable source-sense coordinates and reviewed Card Identities prevent wording or ordering changes from silently creating different notes. The Card Registry preserves canonical GUIDs, while the shared build contracts define deterministic paths and serialized card results used by downstream stages.

tour.title: Review and Promotion
tour.description: Stable identities make fingerprint-bound human review possible: semantic and collocation audits account for source evidence before learner-facing content is approved. The Semantic Registry is promoted only from complete review authorities, and its cutover ADR explains why reviewed bilingual definitions and examples supersede legacy semantic layers. The maintained audit CLI is the operational doorway into this fail-closed workflow.

tour.title: Registry-Driven Build
tour.description: After promotion, the registry builder joins Card Identity with reviewed semantics, collocations, pronunciation, audio, vocabulary membership, and source metadata to construct final Anki notes. Sense simplification resolves and orders evidence before the build command publishes paired JSONL and TXT artifacts. Validation checks identity, field alignment, media, and deterministic parity, while transactional publication prevents partial outputs.

tour.title: EAVM Card Design
tour.description: The validated note fields become two sibling EAVM experiences: an English Recognition card and a Vietnamese-to-English Production card. The visual preview is the CSS source of truth, the templates define each face, and the synchronization helper derives production styling while detecting drift. Reading these together shows how semantic alignment survives all the way into the learner interface.
tour.languageLesson: Anki templates combine HTML, field placeholders, CSS, and embedded JavaScript. Keeping one bounded CSS source region and generating the packaged stylesheet turns visual parity into a testable contract.

tour.title: Deterministic Packaging
tour.description: The built notes and EAVM design are assembled into an APKG under a stable model, field, template, deck, and media contract. Offline archive validation proves the concrete package contents, while content hashes bind the package to every canonical input and later import receipt. The historical top-level packager remains only as a compatibility surface over these maintained modules.

tour.title: Safe Identity Growth
tour.description: Packaging crosses into live Anki through a guarded command that backs up state, migrates the established model, adds only missing canonical identities, synchronizes media, and verifies the result through AnkiConnect. Canonical GUID export proof prevents note counts or filenames from masquerading as identity evidence. When one identity must split, the reviewed transaction and its ADR add rollback, recovery, full source coverage, and idempotency rather than allowing partial registry growth.

tour.title: Regression Safety Net
tour.description: The highest-risk transitions from orchestration through registry build, validation, design synchronization, and live import each have focused regression suites. These tests make the earlier architectural contracts executable, including mandatory import behavior, deterministic artifacts, CSS parity, and preservation of established Anki state.

tour.title: Release Boundary
tour.description: Once focused regressions pass, the read-only Release Guard independently reproduces canonical state and verifies package or live-import evidence without mutating it. Its CLI exposes the three scopes, its tests exercise stale and invalid evidence, and the provenance ADR records why each boundary needs distinct proof. GitHub Actions runs those checks with the full suite across Linux and Windows and finishes by rejecting tracked-file drift.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions YAML separates event triggers, jobs, and ordered steps; OS matrix coverage is especially valuable here because byte-stable canonical output is a cross-platform release requirement.

tour.title: Living Architecture
tour.description: Finish with the documents that keep terminology, operations, tool support, and architectural rationale from drifting apart. CONTEXT.md owns the project vocabulary, AGENTS.md owns operational rules, the documentation index routes durable decisions to ADRs, and the tools guide distinguishes maintained workflows from retired commands. Together they explain where to record the next change after you understand the runtime path.
